binaries.title: csc.sys (Windows Offline Files / Client-Side Caching driver) - feature-gated directory-enumeration double-buffering refactor in CscQueryDirectory

changed_functions.name: CscQueryDirectory (sub_1C0069FF0 unpatched) / CscQueryDirectory (sub_1C006C180 patched) - CSC miniredirector directory-enumeration worker, registered in the dispatch table by CscInitializeDispatchTable (sub_1C008D338, address taken at 0x1C008D544) and reached via the RDBSS MRxQueryDirectory / IRP_MJ_DIRECTORY_CONTROL dispatch
key_changes: ADD: A path gated on Feature_3405715771__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage (sub_1C0007A98 in the PATCHED build) that allocates a scratch buffer of exactly the output-buffer length via ExAllocatePoolWithTag(NonPagedPoolNx (0x200), NumberOfBytes = *(arg1+0x1c8), tag 0x72447343 = 'CsDr') at 0x1C006C8B9, zeroes it (memset 0x1C006C8ED), sets an allocated flag (var_BF=1), swaps it into *(arg1+0x1b8) at 0x1C006C8FA, runs the directory-enumeration writes into it, then memmove's ONLY bytes_written bytes (r13d = *(FOBX+0x6C), 0x1C006CB01) back to the original buffer at 0x1C006CB8C, restores the original pointer at 0x1C006CB99, and frees the scratch via ExFreePoolWithTag(scratch, 0) at 0x1C006CC56 (after memset(FOBX+0x48, 0, 0x40) at 0x1C006CC4C). When the feature is off (0x1C006C8A2) the patched code writes in place, matching the unpatched behavior.

NOTE: In the unpatched build the enumeration writes go directly to the caller's output buffer at *(arg1+0x1b8). The size used, *(arg1+0x1c8) (loaded into r13d at 0x1C006A04D), is that buffer's OWN byte length. The pointer at +0x1b8 and the length at +0x1c8 are a matched pair: both builds compute remaining = length - bytes_written and store it back to +0x1c8 (unpatched 0x1C006A9C0, patched 0x1C006CC03). There is no evidence in either binary that +0x1c8 can exceed the allocation of the buffer at +0x1b8; every write is bounded by that length, and the patched scratch is allocated to exactly that length.

NOTE: The two memset(*(arg1+0x1b8), 0, *(arg1+0x1c8)) sites are output-buffer clears bounded by the buffer's own length. Site #1 (unpatched 0x1C006A23F) is on the FCB-signature/parameter validation FAILURE path (cmp [rbx], 0EC21h fails, or the +0x1F6 check fails; status 0xC000000D / 0xC0000002) and clears the caller's output buffer before returning an error; site #2 (unpatched 0x1C006A616) is the 0x30-flag enumeration branch (test byte [var_38], 30h at 0x1C006A532). Neither is an out-of-bounds write.

NOTE: CscDetermineAbortStatus (sub_1C0016C78) is called in BOTH builds (unpatched 0x1C006A116, patched 0x1C006C2D6); it is not new and is unrelated to the added scratch path. CscQueryDirStitchRemoteBuffer (sub_1C0069AA0) is NOT called by CscQueryDirectory in either build.
NOTE: Register allocation was reshuffled (rsi->r14, r12/r13 reassigned, extra callee-saved register pushed, stack frame grown from 0xf0 to 0x120) to carry the scratch-buffer state.
changed_functions.security_notes: No security-relevant change. CscQueryDirectory (sub_1C0069FF0 unpatched / sub_1C006C180 patched) is the CSC directory-enumeration worker. The field at RX_CONTEXT+0x1b8 is the directory-enumeration output buffer pointer and +0x1c8 is that buffer's byte length; the two are a matched pair. In the unpatched build the enumeration zeroes and populates the buffer using +0x1c8 as the capacity, and every write is bounded by that length. The success path in both builds writes remaining = length - bytes_written back to +0x1c8, confirming the buffer/length pairing. The patched build adds a WIL-feature-gated (Feature_3405715771__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage) path that routes the enumeration writes through a freshly allocated NonPagedPoolNx scratch buffer of EXACTLY the same length (tag 'CsDr' 0x72447343), then copies back only the produced bytes (bytes_written <= length) to the caller's buffer and frees the scratch. This changes no size/allocation relationship: the scratch is the same length as the caller's buffer, and the copy-back is bounded by bytes_written. If the caller's buffer could ever be smaller than the length, the patched copy-back into that same buffer would overflow it as well, so the fix implicitly relies on the pair being consistent - exactly as the unpatched code does. There is no out-of-bounds write, no pool overflow, and no other memory-safety defect being removed. The change is a feature-staged double-buffering refactor (defense-in-depth / staged rollout), gated so that when the feature is off the behavior is identical to the unpatched build. No CWE class and no exploit primitive are justified by the binaries.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
=== UNPATCHED: memset clears the output buffer to ITS OWN length (two sites) ===
; Prologue: load the output-buffer length
mov     r13d, [rsi+1C8h]        ; r13d = output-buffer length  @0x1C006A04D

; FCB signature / parameter validation gates the main path:
mov     eax, 0EC21h
cmp     [rbx], ax               ; FCB signature check
jz      loc_1C006A2B1           ; ok -> main path
mov     edi, 0C000000Dh         ; else STATUS_INVALID_DEVICE_REQUEST

; memset site #1 (error/validation path): zero output buffer to its length  @0x1C006A23F:
mov     r8d, r13d               ; count = length
xor     edx, edx
mov     rcx, [rsi+1B8h]         ; dst = output buffer
call    memset

; memset site #2 (0x30-flag enumeration branch)  @0x1C006A616:
test    byte ptr [rsp+..], 30h  ; @0x1C006A532
jnz     loc_1C006A5F9
mov     r8d, r12d               ; count = length
xor     edx, edx
mov     rcx, [rsi+1B8h]         ; dst = output buffer
call    memset

; enumeration writer, capacity = length  @0x1C006A75F:
mov     r9d, r12d               ; capacity = length
mov     r8, [rsi+1B8h]          ; output buffer
mov     edx, [rsi+1B0h]         ; FileInformationClass
call    QueryDirEnumSet

; success: write remaining length back to +0x1c8 (buffer/length pairing)  @0x1C006A9C0:
mov     eax, r13d              ; length
sub     eax, ecx              ; - bytes_written
mov     [rsi+1C8h], eax       ; +0x1c8 = remaining length

=== PATCHED: added feature-gated same-size scratch + bounded copy-back ===
call    Feature_3405715771__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage  ; @0x1C006C899
test    eax, eax
jnz     loc_1C006C8AB
mov     rbx, [r14+1B8h]        ; feature off: write in place (unpatched behavior)
jmp     loc_1C006C901
; feature on: allocate scratch of exactly `length` bytes:
mov     r8d, 72447343h        ; tag 'CsDr'
mov     rdx, r13              ; NumberOfBytes = length
mov     ecx, 200h             ; NonPagedPoolNx
call    cs:__imp_ExAllocatePoolWithTag  ; @0x1C006C8B9
mov     rbx, rax
mov     r8, r13               ; count = length
xor     edx, edx
mov     rcx, rax
call    memset                ; zero scratch  @0x1C006C8ED
mov     [rsp+..+var_BF], 1     ; temp_allocated = 1
mov     [r14+1B8h], rbx       ; swap scratch into +0x1b8  @0x1C006C8FA
; ... enumeration writes into scratch, capacity = length ...
; copy only produced bytes back, restore original pointer:
mov     r13d, [r13+6Ch]       ; bytes_written from FOBX+0x6C  @0x1C006CB01
call    Feature_3405715771__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage  ; @0x1C006CB69
mov     r8d, r13d             ; count = bytes_written (<= length)
mov     rdx, [rsp+..+Src]     ; src = scratch
mov     rcx, [rsp+..+var_60]  ; dst = original buffer
call    memmove               ; @0x1C006CB8C
mov     [r14+1B8h], rax       ; restore original pointer  @0x1C006CB99
; free scratch:
call    Feature_3405715771__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage  ; @0x1C006CC1F
call    memset                ; memset(FOBX+0x48, 0, 0x40)  @0x1C006CC4C
call    cs:__imp_ExFreePoolWithTag  ; ExFreePoolWithTag(scratch, 0)  @0x1C006CC56

security_findings.function: CscQueryDirectory (sub_1C0069FF0 unpatched / sub_1C006C180 patched, CSC miniredirector directory-enumeration worker)
security_findings.entry_point: IRP_MJ_DIRECTORY_CONTROL (query directory) on a path transiting the CSC (Offline Files) RDBSS miniredirector -> RDBSS MRxQueryDirectory dispatch -> CscQueryDirectory (registered by CscInitializeDispatchTable sub_1C008D338, address taken at 0x1C008D544). This is the normal directory-enumeration path; it is not a corruption trigger.
call_chain: NtQueryDirectoryFile / FindFirstFileW on a directory engaging the CSC miniredirector (user mode)
CSC dispatch table installed by CscInitializeDispatchTable (sub_1C008D338); MRxQueryDirectory entry = CscQueryDirectory (address taken at 0x1C008D544)
memset (buffer at RX_CONTEXT+0x1b8, count = its own length RX_CONTEXT+0x1c8) and QueryDirEnumSet (sub_1C006AF30, capacity = same length) - all bounded by the buffer length
security_findings.vulnerability_class: None. Feature-gated (Feature_3405715771) double-buffering refactor of the directory-enumeration output; no size/allocation mismatch and no out-of-bounds write in either build.
security_findings.description: In both builds, CscQueryDirectory zeroes and populates the directory-enumeration output buffer at RX_CONTEXT+0x1b8 using RX_CONTEXT+0x1c8 as the capacity. The pointer and the length are a matched pair: on success both builds compute remaining = length - bytes_written and store it back to +0x1c8 (unpatched 0x1C006A9C0, patched 0x1C006CC03), and every write (the two memset sites and QueryDirEnumSet) is bounded by that length. The patched build adds a path gated on Feature_3405715771__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage (sub_1C0007A98) that allocates a NonPagedPoolNx scratch buffer of EXACTLY the same length (tag 0x72447343 'CsDr'), performs the enumeration writes into it, then memmove's only the produced bytes (bytes_written = *(FOBX+0x6C), always <= length) back to the caller's buffer and frees the scratch. This changes no bounds relationship: the scratch is the same length as the caller's buffer and the copy-back is bounded by bytes_written. Were the caller's buffer ever smaller than the length, the patched copy-back into it would overflow just as an in-place write would - so the fix relies on the pair being consistent, exactly like the unpatched code. There is no out-of-bounds write being removed. The change is a feature-staged defense-in-depth refactor (double-buffering), not a vulnerability remediation.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Not applicable. There is no memory-safety defect to trigger. The main enumeration path requires a valid redirector FCB (signature 0xEC21 at RX_CONTEXT+0x38) and the 0x20/0x30 flag sub-branch, i.e. an ordinary directory enumeration through the CSC redirector; every write remains bounded by the output-buffer length at +0x1c8.
security_findings.primitive: None. No out-of-bounds write, pool overflow, or other memory-safety primitive is present in either build. The patched scratch buffer is the same length as the caller's buffer and the copy-back is bounded by bytes_written <= length.
security_findings.assembly:
=== UNPATCHED (sub_1C0069FF0) - buffer zeroed to its own length; no OOB ===
mov     r13d, [rsi+1C8h]        ; output-buffer length  @0x1C006A04D
cmp     [rbx], ax               ; ax=0xEC21 FCB signature check  @0x1C006A218
jz      loc_1C006A2B1
mov     edi, 0C000000Dh         ; else STATUS_INVALID_DEVICE_REQUEST
; memset site #1 (error/validation path):
mov     r8d, r13d               ; count = length
xor     edx, edx
mov     rcx, [rsi+1B8h]         ; dst = output buffer
call    memset                  ; @0x1C006A23F
; memset site #2 (0x30-flag enumeration branch, gated at 0x1C006A532):
mov     r8d, r12d               ; count = length
xor     edx, edx
mov     rcx, [rsi+1B8h]
call    memset                  ; @0x1C006A616
; enumeration writer, capacity = length:
mov     r9d, r12d               ; capacity = length
mov     r8, [rsi+1B8h]
mov     edx, [rsi+1B0h]
call    QueryDirEnumSet         ; @0x1C006A75F
; success bookkeeping:
mov     eax, r13d ; sub eax, ecx ; mov [rsi+1C8h], eax  ; remaining length  @0x1C006A9C0

=== PATCHED (sub_1C006C180) - added feature-gated same-size scratch + bounded copy-back ===
call    Feature_3405715771__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage  ; @0x1C006C899
test    eax, eax
jnz     loc_1C006C8AB
mov     rbx, [r14+1B8h]         ; feature off: write in place
jmp     loc_1C006C901
mov     r8d, 72447343h          ; tag 'CsDr'
mov     rdx, r13               ; NumberOfBytes = length
mov     ecx, 200h              ; NonPagedPoolNx
call    cs:__imp_ExAllocatePoolWithTag  ; @0x1C006C8B9
mov     rbx, rax
mov     r8, r13 ; xor edx, edx ; mov rcx, rax
call    memset                  ; zero scratch  @0x1C006C8ED
mov     [rsp+..+var_BF], 1      ; temp_allocated = 1
mov     [r14+1B8h], rbx        ; swap scratch into +0x1b8  @0x1C006C8FA
; ... enumeration writes into scratch, capacity = length ...
mov     r13d, [r13+6Ch]        ; bytes_written from FOBX+0x6C  @0x1C006CB01
mov     r8d, r13d ; mov rdx, [Src] ; mov rcx, [orig]
call    memmove                 ; copy bytes_written back  @0x1C006CB8C
mov     [r14+1B8h], rax        ; restore original pointer  @0x1C006CB99
call    cs:__imp_ExFreePoolWithTag  ; free scratch  @0x1C006CC56

breakpoints.why: Directory-enumeration worker entry. arg1 (rcx) = PRX_CONTEXT. *(rcx+0x1b8) is the output buffer and *(rcx+0x1c8) its byte length; they are a matched pair. Both memset sites use *(rcx+0x1c8) as the count for the buffer at *(rcx+0x1b8), i.e. the buffer's own length. No overflow is expected.

Hit at 0x1C006A23F (error/validation path) and 0x1C006A616 (0x30-flag enumeration branch). In both cases rcx = *(RX_CONTEXT+0x1b8) and r8 (count) = *(RX_CONTEXT+0x1c8) = the buffer's own length; the write stays within the buffer.

Patched-only. When Feature_3405715771 is enabled, this allocates the scratch buffer of NumberOfBytes = length (rdx), tag 'CsDr'. The subsequent memmove at 0x1C006CB8C copies bytes_written (<= length) back to the original buffer.

watch: *(rcx+0x1c8) = output-buffer byte length (paired with +0x1b8; loaded into r13d at 0x1C006A04D)
At each memset: r8 (count) == *(RX_CONTEXT+0x1c8) == the buffer's own length (in-bounds)
Patched: [rsp+..+Src] = scratch buffer; r13d at 0x1C006CB01 = bytes_written = *(FOBX+0x6C) (the memmove count, <= length)
key_offsets: sub_1C0069FF0 @ 0x1C006A04D: 'mov r13d, [rsi+0x1c8]' loads the output-buffer length
0x1C006A218: 'cmp [rbx], ax' (ax=0xEC21) FCB signature check gating the main path
memset site #1 @0x1C006A23F (error/validation path); memset site #2 @0x1C006A616 (0x30-flag branch, gated at 0x1C006A532)
QueryDirEnumSet @0x1C006A75F with capacity = length; success bookkeeping 'remaining = length - bytes_written' @0x1C006A9C0
PATCHED added: ExAllocatePoolWithTag @0x1C006C8B9, scratch memset @0x1C006C8ED, swap-in @0x1C006C8FA, memmove copy-back @0x1C006CB8C, restore @0x1C006CB99, ExFreePoolWithTag @0x1C006CC56
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Not applicable - no memory-safety defect to trigger. An ordinary directory enumeration through the CSC redirector reaches CscQueryDirectory, but every write is bounded by the output-buffer length at +0x1c8 in both builds.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: No out-of-bounds access under Driver Verifier / Special Pool for either build, because the memset and enumeration writes are bounded by the output buffer's own length and the patched scratch is allocated to that same length with a bytes_written-bounded copy-back.
debugging_aids.notes: RX_CONTEXT offsets: +0x38 = FCB pointer (signature 0xEC21 gates the main path), +0x1b0 = FileInformationClass, +0x1b8 = directory-enumeration output buffer, +0x1c8 = that buffer's byte length (a matched pair with +0x1b8). CscDetermineAbortStatus (sub_1C0016C78) is present in BOTH builds (0x1C006A116 / 0x1C006C2D6). CscQueryDirStitchRemoteBuffer (sub_1C0069AA0) is NOT called by CscQueryDirectory. The patched-build address 0x1C0007A98 is Feature_3405715771__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage; the same address in the unpatched build is an unrelated function (CscEaSystemMemFree). Both images use preferred base 0x1C0000000; add the runtime base delta under ASLR.

summary: Only one function differs between the two builds: the CSC directory-enumeration worker CscQueryDirectory (sub_1C0069FF0 unpatched / sub_1C006C180 patched). The patched build adds a code path, gated behind the WIL feature toggle Feature_3405715771__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage (sub_1C0007A98), that routes the directory-enumeration writes through a freshly allocated NonPagedPoolNx scratch buffer of the SAME length as the caller's output buffer (tag 0x72447343 'CsDr'), then copies back only the produced bytes (bytes_written <= length) and frees the scratch. The output-buffer pointer (RX_CONTEXT+0x1b8) and its byte length (RX_CONTEXT+0x1c8) are a matched pair in both builds - both compute remaining = length - bytes_written and store it back to +0x1c8 - and every write is bounded by that length. The scratch is allocated to exactly that length and the copy-back is bounded by bytes_written, so the change alters no size/allocation relationship and introduces or removes no out-of-bounds condition. This is a feature-staged double-buffering refactor (defense-in-depth / staged rollout), not a vulnerability remediation. No CWE class and no exploit primitive are justified by the binaries; severity: none.
